title: Dual power i/o transmitter
abstract: An apparatus is provided which comprises: a first power supply rail to provide a first power supply; second and third power supply rails to provide second and third power supplies, respectively, wherein a voltage level of the first power supply is higher than a voltage level of each of the second and third power supplies; a first driver circuitry coupled to the first power supply rail and the second power supply rail; a second driver circuitry coupled to the third power supply rail, and coupled to the first driver circuitry; and a stack of transistors of N conductivity type coupled to the first power supply rail, and to the second driver circuitry.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. An apparatus comprising: a first power supply rail to provide a first power supply; a second and a third power supply rail to provide second and third power supplies, wherein the first power supply is higher than the second and third power supplies; a first driver circuitry coupled to the first and second power supply rail; a second driver circuitry coupled to the third power supply rail, and coupled to the first driver circuitry via a resistor device; and a stack of transistors of N conductivity type coupled to the first power supply rail, and the second driver circuitry. 2. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the first driver circuitry comprises: a first stack of transistors including: a first transistor and a second transistor coupled in series and having a first common node, wherein the first transistor is coupled to the first power supply rail; and a third transistor coupled to the first common node, wherein the third transistor is coupled to the second power supply rail; and a second stack of transistors of an opposite conductivity type to the first stack of tran sistors, wherein the second stack of transistors includes: a fourth transistor and a fifth transistor coupled in series and having a second common node, wherein the second stack of transistors is coupled in series to the first stack of transistors and having a third common node; and a sixth transistor coupled to the second common node, wherein the sixth tran sistor is coupled to the second power supply rail. 3. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the second driver circuitry comprises: a third stack of transistors including a seventh transistor and an eighth transistor cou pled in series and having a fourth common node, wherein the seventh transistor is cou pled to the third power supply rail; and a fourth stack of transistors of an opposite conductivity type to the third stack of tran sistors, wherein the fourth stack of transistors includes: 

 a ninth transistor and a tenth transistor coupled in series and having a fifth com mon node, wherein the fourth stack of transistors is coupled in series to the third stack of transistors and having a sixth common node; and an eleventh transistor coupled to the sixth common node, wherein the eleventh transistor is coupled to the resistor device. 4. The apparatus of claim 2, wherein: a gate terminal of the first transistor is coupled to a data input; a gate terminal of the second transistor is coupled to a first bias; and a gate terminal of the third transistor is coupled to a first enable input. 5. The apparatus of claim 2, wherein: a gate terminal of the fourth transistor is coupled to a second bias; a gate terminal of the fifth transistor is coupled to a second data input; and a gate terminal of the third transistor is coupled to a second enable input. 6. The apparatus of claim 3, wherein: a gate terminal of the seventh transistor is coupled to a third data input; and a gate terminal of the eighth transistor is coupled to a third bias. 7. The apparatus of claim 3, wherein: a gate terminal of the ninth transistor is coupled to a fourth data input; a gate terminal of the tenth transistor is coupled to a fourth bias; and a gate terminal of the eleventh transistor is coupled to a third enable input. 8. The apparatus of claim 2, wherein the first stack of transistors comprises a p-type transis tor. 9. The apparatus of claim 3, wherein the third stack of transistors comprises a p-type transis tor. 10. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the stack of transistors of N conductivity type are cou pled to the first driver circuitry via the resistor device. 11. The apparatus according to any one of claims 1 to 10, wherein the first driver circuitry comprises a DDR driver circuitry, and the second driver circuitry comprises a LPDDR driver circuitry. 12. A system comprising: a memory; a processor coupled to the memory, the processor including an apparatus according to any one of claims 1 to 11 ; and a wireless interface to allow the processor to communicate with another device. 13. An apparatus comprising: a power supply rail to provide a power supply; a first stack of transistors including: a first transistor and a second transistor coupled in series and having a first common node, wherein the first transistor is coupled to the first power supply rail; and a second stack of transistors of an opposite conductivity type to the first stack of tran sistors, wherein the second stack of transistors includes: a third transistor and a fourth transistor coupled in series and having a second common node, wherein the third transistor is coupled to the power supply rail. 14. The apparatus of claim 13, comprising a third stack of transistors of an opposite conduc tivity type to the first stack of transistors, wherein the third stack of transistors includes: a fifth transistor and a sixth transistor coupled in series and having a third common node, wherein the third stack of transistors is coupled in series to the first stack of transis tors and having fourth and fifth common nodes. 15. A system comprising: a memory; a processor coupled to the memory, the processor including an apparatus according to any one of claims 13 to 14; and a wireless interface to allow the processor to communicate with another device. 16. A method comprising: 

 providing a first power supply to a first power supply rail; providing second and third power supplies to second and third power supply rails, re spectively, wherein the first power supply is higher than the second and third power sup plies; coupling a first driver circuitry to the first and second power supply rail; coupling a second driver circuitry to the third power supply rail; coupling the second driver circuitry to the first driver circuitry via a resistor device; and coupling a stack of transistors of N conductivity type to the first power supply rail, and the second driver circuitry. 17. The method of claim 16, wherein the first driver circuitry comprises: a first stack of transistors including: a first transistor and a second transistor coupled in series and having a first common node, wherein the first transistor is coupled to the first power supply rail; and a third transistor coupled to the first common node, wherein the third transistor is coupled to the second power supply rail; and a second stack of transistors of an opposite conductivity type to the first stack of tran sistors, wherein the second stack of transistors includes: a fourth transistor and a fifth transistor coupled in series and having a second common node, wherein the second stack of transistors is coupled in series to the first stack of transistors and having a third common node; and a sixth transistor coupled to the second common node, wherein the sixth tran sistor is coupled to the second power supply rail. 18. The method of claim 16, wherein the second driver circuitry comprises: a third stack of transistors including a seventh transistor and an eighth transistor cou pled in series and having a fourth common node, wherein the seventh transistor is cou pled to the third power supply rail. 19. The method of claim 17, wherein: a gate terminal of the first transistor is coupled to a data input; a gate terminal of the second transistor is coupled to a first bias; and 

 a gate terminal of the third transistor is coupled to a first enable input. 20. The method of claim 17, wherein: a gate terminal of the fourth transistor is coupled to a second bias; a gate terminal of the fifth transistor is coupled to a second data input; and a gate terminal of the third transistor is coupled to a second enable input.
